name: Dub.co Link Management API
what_it_does: Open-source link management platform with REST API for creating branded short links, tracking click analytics, managing UTM parameters, and generating QR codes. Positioned as developer-first Bitly alternative.
use_cases: Creating and managing campaign tracking links with UTM parameters via API
Bulk link creation for marketing campaigns with consistent branding
Querying link analytics (clicks, countries, devices) for campaign reporting agents
Generating QR codes programmatically for offline-to-online tracking
Managing link redirects dynamically based on user attributes or A/B tests
not_for: Enterprise link security and filtering (use enterprise URL security solutions)
High-volume URL shortening without analytics needs (simpler free tools exist)
Organizations requiring on-premises URL shortening without cloud dependency
best_when: An agent automates marketing link creation, tracks campaign performance, or manages branded short links at scale for growth and marketing workflows.
avoid_when: You need simple URL shortening without analytics — free tools are sufficient.

auth.notes: API keys from Dub dashboard. OAuth2 for third-party app integration. Bearer token in Authorization header. Workspace-scoped tokens. Granular permission scopes for links, analytics, domains, and tags.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pro from $19/month (unlimited links, advanced analytics); Business from $69/month
pricing.notes: Open-source (AGPL-3.0) with self-hosting option. SaaS plans for managed hosting. Very developer-friendly with TypeScript SDK and OpenAPI spec. Growing alternative to Bitly.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec published and comprehensive. TypeScript SDK with full type safety. Error responses are well-structured JSON. Bulk link creation supported. Webhook events for link analytics.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Link creation is idempotent with same key — returns existing link. Bulk operations supported with array of links.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier 25-link limit hits quickly for automation use cases — paid plan likely required
Custom domains must be configured and verified before use in links
Analytics data has 24-48 hour processing delay for some metrics
Self-hosting requires significant setup — SaaS is simpler for most automation use cases

security_score_components.security_notes: AGPL-3.0 open-source — auditable. GDPR compliant. OAuth2 with scoped access. TLS enforced. Self-hosting option for full data control. Active security maintenance by small dedicated team.
